title: How Lucid Went From Near-Term Upside to Major Disappointment
text: Lucid Group (LCID) Earnings Misses and Analyst Downgrades. Earnings Misses. Lucid reported Q1 2026 revenue of $282.5 million versus analyst estimates of $377 million (a 25.1% miss), with adjusted EPS of -$2.82 versus expectations of -$2.30 (a 22.8% miss). The revenue miss was the largest shortfall in more than four years, stemming primarily from a supplier-related complication that delayed February deliveries of the Gravity SUV. Analyst Downgrades. Wall Street's response has been decidedly negative. Morgan Stanley halved its target to $5 (Underweight), TD Cowen cut its target to $7 (Hold), and Benchmark moved from Buy to Hold. Analysts at major firms have responded with a wave of downgrades and lowered price targets. Additional Concerns. Lucid suspended annual production guidance and is undergoing a new-CEO business review. Uncertainty and production adjustments to clear bloated inventory are increasing near-term execution risk. Lucid's track record includes six earnings misses in the last nine quarters.
